title: As DoorDash stock hits lowest price ever, analysts track a slowdown in food delivery
text: The food-delivery business has seen lots of ups and downs this year — shutdowns, layoffs, consolidations — as consumers resume their pre-pandemic ways.

title: DoorDash: 3 Reasons It's A Buy
text: DoorDash's stock is down around 80% from previous highs set last year. Q2 revenue grew at 30% YoY but is expected to slow in the near term. The company is still not profitable as the focus remains on growing market share.

title: DoorDash (DASH) Introduces Solutions to Attract Customers
text: DoorDash (DASH) launches self-serve ad solutions to drive its total orders and marketplace gross order volume.

title: Worried About the Market Sell-Off? Here's 1 Stock to Buy Now, and 1 to Avoid
text: History suggests down markets offer buying opportunities, but investors should be selective about the stocks they choose.

title: Why DoorDash Stock Is Delivering A 4% Gain Today - DoorDash (NYSE:DASH)
text: DoorDash Inc (NYSE: DASH) shares are trading higher Wednesday as the stock rebounds after falling earlier this week on reports the U.S.

title: Federal gig worker proposal tanks Uber, Lyft and DoorDash stocks
text: The stock prices of Uber, Lyft and DoorDash slid on Tuesday after the Department of Labor announced proposed changes to how workers should be classified. The prospective guidance is intended to “combat employee misclassification,” the federal agency said.

title: Uber, Lyft and DoorDash react to Biden's gig worker rule
text: Uber, Lyft and DoorDash reacted Tuesday to the Department of Labor's proposed rule about classifying workers as employees versus independent contractors.

title: DoorDash: Overvalued, But Hold For Strong Long-Term Growth Prospects
text: DoorDash's revenue has grown from $885 million in FY2019 to $4.89 billion in FY2021, resulting in a 3-year CAGR of 156.10%. The company has recently announced its partnerships with top grocers and retailers.

title: Biden Proposal Could Lead to Employee Status for Gig Workers
text: A proposed rule, long awaited by labor activists, would make it harder for companies to classify workers as independent contractors.

title: Proposed labor rule could give millions of gig workers employee status
text: The Biden administration is proposing a new labor rule that could classify millions of gig workers as employees — a move that would challenge the low-cost labor models behind Silicon Valley heavyweights such as Uber, Lyft and DoorDash.

title: ‘Gig work' rule is in Biden administration's crosshairs, sending DoorDash and Lyft stocks to all-time lows
text: The Labor Department on Tuesday proposed a rule that could cut at the heart of the business models of Uber Technologies Inc., Lyft Inc., DoorDash Inc. and other companies that primarily rely on “gig workers,” and could affect the classification of contract workers in many other industries.

title: Uber, Lyft and DoorDash tumble as Biden administration proposes gig-worker rule that eases path to full employee
text: The proposed rule would base the determination of whether to classify a worker as a contractor or employee on a broad assessment.

title: Labor Department Proposal On Gig Workers Hits These Businesses
text: Uber stock, as well as Lyft and DoorDash, dropped on a Labor Department proposal that could reclassify millions of gig workers. The post Labor Department Proposal On Gig Workers Hits These Businesses appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.

title: Uber, Doordash plunge after Labor Department proposes change to gig worker classification
text: The proposed rule sent stocks for gig companies like DoorDash, Lyft and Uber down.

title: Why DoorDash Stock Is Sliding - DoorDash (NYSE:DASH)
text: DoorDash Inc (NYSE: DASH) shares are trading lower Tuesday on reports the U.S. Department of Labor has released a proposal to make companies reclassify independent contractors as employees.

title: DoorDash (DASH) Introduces Feature Drinks With DoubleDash
text: DoorDash (DASH) unveils new feature Drinks with DoubleDash to allow customers to order food from one place and drinks from another place in the same vicinity in a single order.

title: DoorDash now lets you bundle food from one place and alcohol from another into a single order
text: DoorDash is introducing a new feature called “Drinks with DoubleDash” that lets you order food from one place and drinks from another all in a single order with no additional delivery fee or order minimum.
